event_text: Bohemia is formally recognised as part of the Holy Roman Empire.
Vratislaus II becomes the first king of Bohemia.
The Golden Bull of Sicily confirms the royal title and privileges of Bohemia.
Charles University is founded in Prague.
The Golden Bull recognises the King of Bohemia as an imperial elector.
Jan Hus is executed at the Council of Constance.
The Hussite Wars reshape religious and political life in Bohemia.
The Habsburgs inherit the Bohemian crown after the Battle of Mohács.
The Defenestration of Prague helps trigger the Thirty Years War.
Bohemian rebels are defeated at the Battle of White Mountain.
The War of the Austrian Succession brings conflict to the Bohemian lands.
Revolutionary unrest reaches Prague during the European revolutions.
The Czech lands remain in the Austrian half of Austria-Hungary after the Compromise.
Czechoslovakia declares independence from Austria-Hungary.
The Munich Agreement gives the Sudetenland to Nazi Germany.
Nazi Germany occupies the Czech lands and creates the Protectorate of Bohemia and Moravia.
Reinhard Heydrich is assassinated in Prague and Nazi reprisals follow.
The Prague uprising occurs as the Second World War ends in Europe.
The Communist Party takes power in Czechoslovakia.
The Prague Spring is suppressed by Warsaw Pact forces.
The Velvet Revolution ends communist rule in Czechoslovakia.
The Czech Republic and Slovakia become separate states.
The Czech Republic joins NATO.
The Czech Republic joins the European Union.
